fields.description:
# op-cert: trusted gate service ŌĆö authoritative-state fetch, per-kind dispatch, async job API (+ client command)

## Why
For high assurance, a developer routes a gate run to a trusted environment that fetches
authoritative state ITSELF (ticket state from the tickets branch; code at the review remote's
`main` tip), runs the gate, and returns an Ed25519-signed `rebar.opcert.v1` cert ŌĆö so
client-supplied inputs cannot forge a pass (the SLSA non-falsifiable-provenance principle). This
story builds the **service and its client half only**; AWS IaC + the real deploy are the sibling
"AWS IaC + deploy" story, and the signing behavior itself ships in the "producer signing seam"
story this depends on. All design forks are settled in the epic's "Producer-signing &
compute-runtime design resolution" section ŌĆö this story implements, it does not decide.

**Hard dependencies (not yet on `main`):** the op-cert substrate consumed here
(`signing.sign_opcert_manifest`, `src/rebar/attest/opcert.py`, `trusted_env.py`, `sshsig.sign` ŌĆö
Gerrit changes 649/650/660, in review) and the producer signing seam story must land first;
mentions of them below are forward references to that stack, enforced by this ticket's
`depends_on` links. `src/rebar/review_bot/app.py` (the pattern mirrored) IS on `main` today.

## What
**Service** (`src/rebar/opcert_service/`, library-mode: rebar imported behind a thin FastAPI app ŌĆö
the `review_bot/app.py` software pattern; NOT the stdio `rebar-mcp`):
- **Async job API** (gate runs take 30sŌĆōminutes; no sync request can hold): `POST /opcert/jobs`
  validates, enqueues on an in-process `asyncio.Queue`, returns **202 `{job_id}`**;
  `GET /opcert/jobs/{job_id}` returns the job record. Single background worker drains serially
  under a bounded per-run timeout. Job state is in-memory (a restart loses queued jobs; clients
  re-submit ŌĆö cert minting is idempotent).
- **Request schema**: `{"ticket_id": str, "kind": "completion-verifier"|"plan-review"}` ŌĆö ONLY
  identifiers. Any other client field (a `material_fingerprint`, a `commit`, an `env_id`) is
  IGNORED; the server derives every authoritative value itself.
- **Authoritative fetch**: the worker fetches the tickets branch (ticket state) and the review
  remote's `main` tip (code) with its own read-only credentials, sets
  `merged_log_commit = <fetched main HEAD sha>`, and computes the material fingerprint itself.
- **Per-kind dispatch + native verdicts (no unified enum)**: `plan-review` ŌåÆ
  `rebar.llm.review_plan(...)` (verdict `PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE`); `completion-verifier` ŌåÆ
  `rebar.llm.verify_completion(...)` (verdict `PASS|FAIL`). A raised
  `LLMError`/`LLMUnavailableError` maps to the job's transport status ŌĆö never a fabricated
  verdict. Response/job schema:
  `{status: "queued"|"running"|"completed"|"error", kind, verdict: <native enum>|null,
  envelope: <encoded DSSE>|null, material_fingerprint, merged_log_commit, error: {class, message}|null}`
  ŌĆö `envelope` present ONLY on a `PASS` verdict.
- **Signing = the producer seam, once.** The gate producers mint the op-cert with the ambient
  environment key (the producer-seam story); the handler does NOT sign separately and does NOT
  pass `sign=False`. The server's environment: `REBAR_OPCERT_ENV_ID` names its principal; the
  Ed25519 private key is fetched from AWS Secrets Manager at signing time, **materialized to a
  0600 `mkstemp` temp file** (SSHSIG signing shells `ssh-keygen -Y sign -f <path>` and needs a
  file path), and **deleted in a `finally`** ŌĆö never persisted. The key MUST be passphrase-free
  (`ssh-keygen -Y sign` is non-interactive). The legacy HMAC environment key is never provisioned
  in the server.
- **Stateless / store-read-only**: the server writes NOTHING to the tickets branch; it returns the
  envelope and the client persists it. Endpoint authn (SigV4) is terminated at API Gateway (the
  IaC story); the app additionally requires a shared-secret header as defense in depth, matching
  `review_bot`'s token check.
- **Container image**: `infra/compose/Dockerfile.opcert` (build context = repo root, per the
  review_bot Dockerfile pattern) installing **OpenSSH Ōēź 8.9** and running
  `uvicorn rebar.opcert_service.app:app`.

**Client** (`src/rebar/_commands/remote_cert.py`, CLI `rebar remote-cert <ticket-id> <kind>`):
- Reads `verify.opcert_remote_url` (new `VerifyConfig` key, `str | None = None`; unset ŌåÆ clear
  error; nothing remote is ever required for local operation). SigV4-signs the request with
  ambient AWS credentials, submits the job, polls to a terminal status, and on `PASS` **persists
  the returned envelope + bound fields as the `SIGNATURE` event** (the same event shape
  `sign_opcert_manifest` appends; auto-pushed with the tickets branch). Safe because the envelope
  is self-authenticating ŌĆö tampering with the bound values breaks the signature the merge gate
  (`rebar verify-opcert`) verifies. Non-PASS / error ŌåÆ non-zero exit with the job's error class.

## Scope
- In: the FastAPI service, dispatch/verdict mapping, authoritative fetch, Secrets-Manager key
  materialization, Dockerfile, the `remote-cert` client command, `verify.opcert_remote_url`.
- Out: Terraform/API Gateway/ECS/Secrets-Manager resources + the real deploy + live round-trip
  (the "AWS IaC + deploy" story); the producer signing seam itself (dependency); HMAC removal
  (8f1d).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `POST /opcert/jobs` ACKs 202 `{job_id}` and `GET /opcert/jobs/{id}` reaches a terminal
      status via the background worker; a test drives the round-trip against a fake authoritative
      source with the request/response schemas above.
- [ ] Integrity test (the load-bearing one): a request carrying a **doctored
      `material_fingerprint`** and a doctored `commit` is accepted, the extra fields are ignored,
      and the signed op-cert's bound `material_fingerprint`/`merged_log_commit` equal the values
      the server derived from its own fetch ŌĆö a client cannot influence any bound value.
- [ ] Per-kind verdict mapping: tests assert `plan-review` jobs surface
      `PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE` natively, `completion-verifier` jobs surface `PASS|FAIL`, a
      raised `LLMError` yields `status="error"` with `verdict=null`, and `envelope` is non-null
      ONLY on `PASS` (BLOCK/FAIL/INDETERMINATE/error all return `envelope: null`).
- [ ] Key materialization: a test asserts the Secrets-Manager secret is written to a 0600
      `mkstemp` file for the `ssh-keygen -Y sign` invocation and that NO key file remains after
      signing (including on a raised exception ŌĆö the `finally` path).
- [ ] The server appends no tickets-branch event: a test asserts the store is unchanged after a
      completed job; `rebar remote-cert` persists the returned envelope as a `SIGNATURE` event
      that `verify_signature`/`rebar verify-opcert` then certify, and a tampered envelope is
      rejected (`mismatch`).
- [ ] Offline default preserved: a test asserts every local op-cert sign/verify path works with
      `verify.opcert_remote_url` unset and no network ŌĆö the remote path is opt-in and its absence
      never blocks a local operation.
- [ ] `infra/compose/Dockerfile.opcert` installs OpenSSH Ōēź 8.9 and starts the service; a test
      asserts the Dockerfile declares both.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green.
